sourceMovie.title: Hereafter
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Hereafter works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and fantasy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: What Dreams May Come
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Hereafter clicked for you, What Dreams May Come (1998) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and fantasy, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Hereafter clicked for you, Horns (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and fantasy, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Hereafter?
faq.answer: Try What Dreams May Come, Horns, City of Angels—each shares DNA with Hereafter while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Hereafter?
faq.answer: Hereafter (2010) is best described as Drama, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Hereafter worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
